intervention: Patients undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty (THA) or total knee arthroplasty (TKA)
indication: revision arthroplasty (total hip arthroplasty (THA) and total knee arthroplasty (TKA))
comparator: hypothetical scenario without the LROI (no registry-related prevention of revisions occurs and revision rates remain unchanged); 0% reduction in revision rates (i.e., no effect of the LROI on revisions); same as in the threshold analyses, translated to the hospital level; observed performance of outlier hospitals relative to a benchmark defined by the upper 99% control limit of the national distribution of risk-adjusted 1-year revision rates; hypothetical situation in which 1-year revision rates remain at historically higher (or lower) levels observed in earlier calendar years

document: The Higher Hospital Costs of Revision Total Hip Arthroplasty by Diagnosis: Analysis of Cost Drivers and Periprosthetic Joint Infection Treatment Pathways.
authors: Aaron S. Hammat | Taisha D'Apollonio | Renjy Nelson | David Campbell | Boopalan Ramasamy | L. Bogdan Solomon | Emmanuel S. Gnanamanickam | Stuart A. Callary
intervention: Patients undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty (pTHA) or revision total hip arthroplasty (rTHA) at a tertiary referral hospital (between January 2014 and June 2022). Overall rTHA cohort consists of 396 patients who underwent 529 EoCs. Mean age 67.7 years (range 21-97). 54% female, 46% male.
indication: Revision total hip arthroplasty (THA)
outcome_measure: Median hospital costs; Median rTHA cost per EoC ($45165); pTHA ($25615, p < 0.05); rTHA cost by diagnosis: dislocation ($37072), loosening ($47708), periprosthetic fractures ($52521), PJI ($65325); PJI treatment pathways median cost: two-stage rTHA ($123688), single-stage rTHA ($63261), debridement and implant retention ($51760).

Revision of total hip arthroplasty; both components, with or without autograft or allograft
Revision of total hip arthroplasty; acetabular component only, with or without autograft or allograft
